item.title: Nikon D500 Cheat Sheet
item.description: Disclaimer: This sheet is being continuously updated. Quick Reference: Astrophotography: EM: M or bulb SS: 10” (higher allows for longer exposure, but obey the 500 rule. f/stop: 1.8 ISO: 1600 Enable live view on camera, focus on brightest object possible. (Use highest possible shutter speed & ISO values here). Exposure Mode: P (Program): D500 selects basic exposure settings, but allows for user modification of all values while still maintaining metered exposure (different light values for different areas of image depending on relative brightness) S (Shutter-priority): Useful to modify shutter speed to create either blur or exposure effects.

item.title: Notes
item.description: Physics 161 Lecture 25 - Work in 1-D Lecture 26 - Work in Multiple Dimensions Lecture 25 - Work in 1-D Lecture 26 - Work in Multiple Dimensions Lecture 27 - More on Energy Lecture 28 - Conservation of Momentum Lecture 29 - Elastic Collisions in 1 & 2 Dimensions Lecture 30 - Linking Momentum & Force Lecture 31 - Universal Gravitation Lecture 32 - Orbits & Kepler's Laws Lecture 33 - Reference Frames Summary of Previous Lectures Equations Sheet Physics 162 Lecture 1 - Introduction Lecture 2 - Charge & Coulomb's Law Lecture 3 - The Electric Field Lecture 4 - Electric Field from Continuous Charge Distributions Underwood's Chapter 23 Notes Lecture 5 - Field Lines and Flux Lecture 8 - Conductors Continued Lecture 9 - Electric Potential V Lecture 10 - Energy, Potential and Point Charges Lecture 11 - Flat Earth Gaussian Surface Lecture 12 - Energy & Potential of Point Charge Configurations Lecture 13 - Potential Inside Conductors & More Regarding Capacitors Lecture 14 - Capacitors in Series & Parallel Lecture 15 - Kirchoff's Law Lecture 16 - Kirchoff's and Ohm's Laws in Practice Lecture 17 - Power in Circuits Electrical Engineering 110 Studying Engineering: A Road Map to a Rewarding Career Miscellaneous Notes on Learning Physics and Science Good Theoretical Physicist
